host: i7-11800H (16T) / RTX 3060 Laptop 6 GiB / Win11 / driver 576.83

camelid_q4_k_m_gpu_resident_decode.prompt: Write a short paragraph about the ocean.
camelid_q4_k_m_gpu_resident_decode.note: Decode-only tok/s. Resident at ~4.92 GB peak in 6 GiB.

llamacpp_q4_k_m_cpu.note: CPU-ONLY. This box's llama.cpp build has no ggml-cuda.dll, so a GPU-vs-GPU comparison is not possible here.

honest_framing.head_to_head: NONE — camelid number is GPU-resident, the only local llama.cpp number is CPU. Different backends; do not read 19.44 vs 12.35 as a speedup ratio.
honest_framing.gpu_vs_gpu_blocked: Requires a CUDA-enabled llama.cpp build (absent). Deferred.
honest_framing.q8_vs_q4km_same_model_blocked: Qwen3-4B-Q8_0 is not on disk (only Llama-3.2-3B-Q8_0). The byte-ratio prize (Q4_K_M ~2.5 GB vs Q8_0 ~4.3 GB) is most visible on CPU decode (Phase 2) and as VRAM footprint on GPU; a same-model Q8 vs Q4_K_M GPU tok/s comparison is deferred to a Q8_0 download.
honest_framing.gpu_prize_realized_now: VRAM footprint: 4.92 GB resident peak lets the full 4B model run GPU-resident in 6 GiB with headroom; the Q8_0 4B is tighter. The decode-speed prize is bandwidth-bound and lands on the CPU lane in Phase 2.
